title: Safety Portal form catalog manifest
description: Git-committed source of truth for the ACTIVE form set, the current version per identity, the parent/variant grouping, and the display order. Phase-2 (the admin form editor) writes this manifest; slice 1b flips the SPA picker (src/forms/registry.ts) and the Python PDF renderer (safety_reports/form_pdf.py load_definition) to READ it instead of deriving everything from the all-bundled glob. The form *definitions* stay one-JSON-per-form under safety_portal/forms/<form_code>.json (the rendering contract consumed by all three renderers, byte-unchanged); this manifest is the active-set / version / order overlay layered on top. INVARIANT (red-team C1): definition files are append-only on disk — edit/retire/delete flip THIS manifest, never delete a .json, so every historical form_code resolves forever (filed AND in-flight submissions). The manifest lives at safety_portal/ (NOT safety_portal/forms/) on purpose: registry.ts and tests/test_form_definitions.py glob forms/*.json and would otherwise mis-ingest the manifest as a form definition.

manifest_version.description: Format version of THIS manifest (data versioning). Bump on any breaking shape change so readers can reject an unknown version rather than silently mis-parse. Currently 1.

parents.description: Form-type parents, each one row of the form-type dropdown. A parent with a single null-variant form renders that form directly; a parent with >1 variant shows the 3rd picklist of variant_labels.

parent_form_code.description: The form-type parent key (e.g. 'jha', 'equipment-preinspection'); matches every member definition's parent_form_code.

name.description: Operator-facing parent label shown in the form-type dropdown.

display_order.description: Ascending sort position among parents. Unique across parents.

category.description: Workflow id — the form-type's workflow (Safety / Progress / …). OPTIONAL: readers (registry.ts, shared/form_category.py) default an absent category to the workflows.json `default`. The valid SET is single-sourced in safety_portal/workflows.json and enforced at the two runtime C3 layers (worker/publishValidation.ts validateCategory + safety_reports/publish_manifest.apply_publish) — a JSON Schema can't import the registry, so this is a pattern, not an enum. Set by the form-builder workflow selector on create + recategorize.

forms.description: Variant identities under this parent. A no-variant parent has EXACTLY ONE form with variant_label null; a variant parent has ALL non-null variant_labels — no mixing (mirrors registry.ts formCatalog()'s binary branch).

identity.description: Version-independent stable key = a form_code minus its -vN suffix (e.g. 'jha', 'equipment-skid-steer'). EDIT bumps the version on a fixed identity; ADD-VERSION / CREATE-FROM-BLANK mint a NEW identity. Globally unique across the manifest.

variant_label.description: 3rd-picklist label (e.g. 'Skid Steer'); null for a no-variant parent. Mirrors the definition file's variant_label. minLength constrains the string case only — an empty-string variant_label renders a blank, unselectable 3rd-picklist row (registry.ts treats '' as a present variant).

status.description: active = appears in the PM picker; retired = soft-removed (definition file + version history retained; reversible un-retire). The manifest status is the ONLY active-set gate.

current_version.description: The version PMs currently fill. MUST equal the version of current_form_code and appear in versions[].

current_form_code.description: Resolved form_code of the current version (= identity + '-v' + current_version). MUST resolve to a definition file at safety_portal/forms/<code>.json.

versions.description: Every version ever published for this identity (append-only; retained for filed/in-flight submissions, rollback, and the Box history archive).

form_code.description: = identity + '-v' + version; resolves to a definition file under safety_portal/forms/.

display_order.description: Ascending sort position among the variants WITHIN this parent. Unique within the parent.
